x-enumeration-literals.name: Cardholder
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The individual or business that initiated the payment using a card and later disputes the transaction.

Comment:
Example:
A customer disputes a €200 online purchase because the goods were never delivered.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Issuing Bank
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The bank that issued the payment card (credit or debit) to the cardholder.

Comment:
Example:
The customer's bank processes the dispute and sends the chargeback request to the network.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Merchant
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The business or seller that accepted the card payment for goods or services.

Comment:
Example:
An online store provides shipping proof to contest the chargeback.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Acquirer
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The bank or payment institution that processes card payments on behalf of the merchant.

Comment:
Example:
The merchants payment processor forwards the chargeback case.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Card Network
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The payment network that connects issuing banks and acquiring banks and governs chargeback rules.

Comment:
Examples: Visa, Mastercard, American Express.
